name: URETEROPELVIC JUNCTION OBSTRUCTION
definition: congenital partial functional / anatomic obstruction at junction of renal pelvis + proximal
dominant_finding: anteroposterior diameter of renal pelvis ≥ 10 mm
discriminating_features: vessel to lower renal pole (in 25-39% of adult patients): anterior to UPJ (90-
draped over lower pole vessel as inverted teardrop shape
proximal ureter hooking over lower pole vessel

general: vessel to lower renal pole (in 25-39% of adult patients): anterior to UPJ (90-
draped over lower pole vessel as inverted teardrop shape
proximal ureter hooking over lower pole vessel
broad tangential sharply defined extrinsic compression (in arterial crossing)
longitudinal striae of redundant mucosa (in dehydrated state)
late changes: unilateral renal enlargement, diminished opacification, wasting of kidney
caliceal crescents surrounding dilated collecting system
eccentric exit of ureter from dilated renal pelvis
accurate detection of crossing vessels (100% PPV) during late arterial phase
NUC: progressive enlargement of renal pelvis + branching infundibula + calices
anteroposterior diameter of renal pelvis ≥ 10 mm
large unilocular fluid collection (severely dilated collecting system)
